frameworks.name: state-unspecific functional brain networks
frameworks.one_line: individual variation in connectivity shared across task and rest states

frameworks.name: Common Neural Modes (CNMs)
frameworks.one_line: data-driven modes capturing trait-like connectivity across multiple states

frameworks.name: state-specific brain network characterization
frameworks.one_line: traditional view that connectivity is largely defined by task state

frameworks.name: individual differences in functional connectivity
frameworks.one_line: fMRI-based neural substrates of behavioral and cognitive variation

core_question: Can state-unspecific whole-brain functional connectivity patterns be identified and do they predict stable individual traits?
key_assumptions: individual variability in connectivity has both state-specific and state-unspecific components
whole-brain connectivity patterns generalize across task and resting states
stable cognitive traits relate to invariant connectivity structure
manipulations: eight task and resting states aggregated from a large fMRI database
dimensions of features used to extract modes
association of modes with cognitive measures and life outcomes
key_findings: three Common Neural Modes were robustly extracted across feature dimensions
each CNM preferentially correlated with distinct cognitive measures
associations emerged unsupervised from connectivity data alone
CNMs predicted life outcomes including income and life satisfaction
interpretations: state-unspecific connectivity modes reflect stable individual traits
trait-relevant brain organization is invariant across cognitive states
